title: Bytes, Text I/O, Encodings, Newlines, and Cleanup · Traceback Repair Bay
summary: A preserved Bookbot run contains one plausible path, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, reporting, CLI, testing, performance, packaging, security, or release defect; identify the first failed boundary before changing code. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in explicit text-I/O boundary and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Distinguish raw bytes, decoded str values, code points, grapheme clusters, and serialized output at every boundary.
Choose and document UTF-8 or another declared encoding instead of relying on locale-dependent defaults.
Select strict rejection, replacement, ignore, or surrogate handling from the stakeholder evidence duty and expose data loss.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Bytes Text Boundary
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Bookbot team handles case 8750 during a large-file migration rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to decode bytes under an explicit UTF-8 and cleanup contract without hiding malformed input; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects; then install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. steps.title: Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Context Cleanup
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: bookbot-context-cleanup-1u48do" that uses case 7194 to decode bytes under an explicit UTF-8 and cleanup contract without hiding malformed input. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Context Cleanup.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Context Cleanup into observable performance evidence. For Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Context Cleanup, keep Context Cleanup connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-context-cleanup-1u48do
# Competency: bookbot-context-cleanup.
# Case 7194: a digital-humanities corpus revision.
# Operating constraint: retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity.
# Changed case: split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte.
def evidence_bookbot_context_cleanup_1u48do(byte_count: int = 120, decoded_characters: int = 112, encoding: str = "utf-8", decode_errors: int = 0, handle_closed: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1u48do = "1u48do-od84u1-7194"
    assert evidence_variant_1u48do
    evidence_axes_1u48do = (
        "corpus1u48do",
        "policyod84u1",
        "revision7194",
        "command1u48do7194",
        "reportod84u17194",
        "test71941u48do",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1u48do)) == 6
    if byte_count < 0 or decoded_characters < 0:
        return False, byte_count, "io-count"
    if encoding.casefold() != "utf-8":
        return False, decoded_characters, "encoding-policy"
    if decode_errors > 0:
        return False, decode_errors, "decode-rejected"
    if not handle_closed:
        return False, byte_count, "cleanup"
    assert byte_count >= decoded_characters
    return True, decoded_characters, "decoded-text"
